input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <MSTest>
    <AssemblyResolution>
      <Directory useLoadContext="true" />
    </AssemblyResolution>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCodeCoverageDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. I've created `local.runsettings` that maps your three settings:

| `.testsettings` | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>` |
| `<AssemblyResolution>` + `useLoadContext` | `<MSTest><AssemblyResolution>` section |
| CodeCoverage `DataCollector` (uri v1.0) | CodeCoverage `DataCollector` (uri v2.0) |

**To use it**, either:
- Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or add `<RunSettingsFilePath>local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>` to your `.csproj` `<PropertyGroup>`

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` once confirmed working.
